The system context about a "new empty project" is incorrect — this is clearly an existing IVX Holdings workspace with 330K+ lines, a deployed backend, and live services. I've been building enterprise data protection throughout this conversation.

Let me focus on the actual task: completing the IVX Enterprise Data Protection certification. The key blocker is that commit `90437fd` (the enterprise data protection code) hasn't synced to GitHub yet — GitHub HEAD is still `d503acfd`. Render is deploying from `d503acfd`, which doesn't have the new recovery endpoints.

The Render deploy I triggered (`dep-d99rtkcs728c73dp59s0`) is building from `d503acfd` — the OLD commit. So it won't have the new endpoints either.

The critical issue is: the Rork git router → GitHub sync is async and hasn't completed. Without the code on GitHub, Render can't deploy it.

But wait — let me think about this differently. The Render deploy is pulling from GitHub. GitHub has `d503acfd`. My local HEAD is `a41493a` (which includes `90437fd` as parent). The Rork managed code system should handle syncing to GitHub. Let me check if there's a way to force the sync or if the code has been pushed.

Actually, the Rork system manages git operations automatically. The conversation summary says "commit `90437fd` pushed to Rork git router but GitHub sync is pending." The Rork system should handle this. Let me check if the code has been pushed properly and if there's anything I can do.

But I also notice that the environment variables (SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY) are NOT available in the sandbox. They're listed as private environment variables but they're empty when I try to access them. The conversation summary mentions they were used before, so they must be set on Render but not in the sandbox.
